Document:
# Pathos Of The Law [bold]Space Law is a tool used to benefit the sector, not a cudgel.[/bold] [bullet] The greater good of the station is to be considered during sentencing [bullet] Space Law is designed to protect the station and crew, not to hinder them. [bullet] Station-Wide emergencies are to take priority over enforcing Space law. [bullet] Roleplay fun is to take priority over all others.
# Modifiers
[color=black]Code[/color]
[color=black]Crime[/color]
[color=black]Description[/color]
[color=black]Adjustments[/color]
[color=black]M01[/color]
[color=black][bold]Good Samaritan Clause[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Crimes committed in the interest of saving life, property, or preventing a disaster may have their sentences reduced, voided, or expunged.[/color]
[color=black]Voiding of charges.[/color]
[color=black]M02[/color]
[color=black][bold]Elevated Sentencing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]The scope of the crime(s), or repetitive behavior committed, warrants the charge be elevated to a higher offense for punishment in increments of one level per instance.[/color]
[color=black]Adjustments are to be decided by the HoS, Warden, or Captain.[/color]
[color=black]M03[/color]
[color=black][bold]Self Defense[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Actions taken to defend oneself from an assailant may have sentences reduced, voided, or expunged if the officer(s) involved deem that use of force reasonable.[/color]
[color=black]Voiding of charges.[/color]
[color=black]M04[/color]
[color=black][bold]Non Compos Mentis[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Those of unstable mind should be referred to medical treatment or, if required, forced cryogenic storage.[/color]
[color=black]Forced medical treatment or cryogenic storage.[/color]
[color=black]M05[/color]
[color=black][bold]Griseo Maris[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Coordinated behavior by those with no clear assigned duty, which interrupts the normal function of any one department.[/color]
[color=black]Severity increased by one level.[/color]
[color=black]M06[/color]
[color=black][bold]Resisting Arrest[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly resisting attempts at legal detention by members of the security team.[/color]
[color=black]20% Punishment increase.[/color]
[color=black]M06[/color]
[color=black][bold]Resisting Arrest[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Knowingly resisting attempts at legal detention by members of the security team.[/color]
[color=black]20% Punishment increase.[/color]
[color=black]M07[/color]
[color=black][bold]Escaped Prisoner[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Someone who has escaped their cell should be recaptured and returned to the brig.[/color]
[color=black]Reset of the time of imprisonment, if prisoner is in permanent confinement Cryogenic storage.[/color]
[color=black]M08[/color]
[color=black][bold]Attempted Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Anyone that tries to commit a Felony or below offense but fails to achieve it, is to be charged under the relevant crime while recording that it was an attempt.[/color]
[color=black]Charged for the same crime, with a 5% decrease in punishment.[/color]
[color=black]M09[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hate Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black]If the crime is committed to cause fear, injury, or other harm due to racial reasons, spiritual, physical, sexual orientation, gender identity, or other reasons that could be classified as a hate crime.
This can not be applied to the crime of discriminatory language, as it is implied.[/color]
[color=black]50% time increase.[/color]
[color=black]M10[/color]
[color=black][bold]Colonial Command Directive[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To be issued a pardon/sentence modification by Colonial Command. Colonial Command may waive sections of Space Law. Irrespective of the original crime, if a pardon or modification to a sentence is issued, it is to be followed immediately.[/color]
[color=black]Immediate release or modification.[/color]
[color=black]M11[/color]
[color=black][bold]Medical Attention[/bold][/color]
[color=black]A sick or injured prisoner requires medical aid. Prisoners have the right to medical aid if injured or sick. In cases of self-harm, aid is discretionary by security. The timer is to continue running during medical aid being provided.[/color]
[color=black]Running timer.[/color]
[color=black]M11[/color]
[color=black][bold]Medical Attention[/bold][/color]
[color=black]A sick or injured prisoner requires medical aid. Prisoners have the right to medical aid if injured or sick. In cases of self-harm, aid is discretionary by security. The timer is to continue running during medical aid being provided.[/color]
[color=black]Running timer.[/color]
[color=black]M12[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hostile Brainwashing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To be under the mind control of a Cult, a head revolutionary, a vampire, or other mind-controlling circumstances. Brainwashed persons are to be detained non-lethally if possible and deconverted. Once deconverted, they are to be released.[/color]
[color=black]Deconversion and release.[/color]
[color=black]M13[/color]
[color=black][bold]Cooperation with Security[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To completely comply and work with security during detainment, Search, or arrest. Such as willingly being put into cuffs without causing disruption, or actively turning oneself in. In cases of Capital crimes, It would lower punishment to Permanent Confinement.[/color]
[color=black]25% punishment decrease.[/color]
# Infraction These crimes tend to have only fines as punishments.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]003[/color]
[color=black][bold]Superficial Vandalism[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To deface the station or property of another individual.[/color]
[color=black]Force repairing [bold]OR[/bold] 15000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]006[/color]
[color=black][bold]Possession, Drugs[/bold][/color]
[color=black]The possession of illegal drugs in quantities appropriate for personal use.[/color]
[color=black]Confiscation of illegal drugs, 25000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]007[/color]
[color=black][bold]Disorderly Conduct[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To conduct oneself in a way unsuitable for the retention of one’s position, When sentenced along with M02 results in demotion.[/color]
[color=black]Brig until 25000 speso fine paid.[/color]
[color=black]008[/color]
[color=black][bold]Creating a Hazard[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To create a situation that may lead to the harm of others during normal operation in that department.[/color]
[color=black]Forced removal of the situation, 15000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]011[/color]
[color=black][bold]Disruptive Comms[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly disrupt or needlessly populate a radio frequency with the intent to be a nuisance to users of that frequency. If done whilst brigged, the right to a headset is voided.[/color]
[color=black]Brig until fine paid, unless under M02, 10000 speso fine.[/color]
# Minor These crimes tend to have only brig time as punishments.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]101[/color]
[color=black][bold]Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To use physical force against someone without the apparent intent to kill them.[/color]
[color=black]Restraining and separation of individuals, 5 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]102[/color]
[color=black][bold]Pickpocketing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To steal items from another person.[/color]
[color=black]Return of object(s), 5 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]103[/color]
[color=black][bold]Minor Vandalism[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To damage, destroy, or permanently deface non-critical furniture, vendors, or personal property.[/color]
[color=black]Work duty to repair damage. Brig time according to the damage and repairs done.[/color]
[color=black]104[/color]
[color=black][bold]Vigilantism[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To perform the responsibilities and duties of the security department without approval or due cause to act.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]106[/color]
[color=black][bold]Illegal Distribution[/bold][/color]
[color=black]The possession of dangerous or illegal drugs/equipment in a quantity greater than that which is reasonable for personal consumption.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]107[/color]
[color=black][bold]Disturbing the Peace[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly organize a movement that disrupts the normal operations of a department.[/color]
[color=black]Warning to disperse, up to 10 minutes of brig time, depending on how easily the illegal protest is dispersed.[/color]
[color=black]108[/color]
[color=black][bold]Criminal Negligence[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To be negligent in one’s duty to an extent that it may cause harm, illness, or other negative effects to another. This includes the reckless operation of a craft.[/color]
[color=black]Simple Negligence does not result in death, and should only have a sentence of 5 minutes.[/color]
[color=black]109[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To be in an area which a person has either not purposefully been admitted to, does not have access, or has been asked to leave by someone a part of the department.[/color]
[color=black]Non-resisting offenders without damage or theft may be released without punishment. Resisting offenders should be sentenced, minimum 5 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]110[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking & Entering[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To trespass into an area using a method of forcible entry, regardless of destructiveness.[/color]
[color=black]Damage caused in entry should be reflected by brig timer. Minimum 5 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]111[/color]
[color=black][bold]Discriminatory Language[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To use language that demeans, generalizes, or otherwise de-personifies the individual to whom it is targeted.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]112[/color]
[color=black][bold]Fine Evasion[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To purposefully avoid or refuse to pay a legal fine, Or to be unable to be able to pay a fine.[/color]
[color=black]Reasonable time & notification should be provided to offender. If fine is blatantly opposed or ignored after multiple warnings, time in brig equal to 15 seconds per 1000 spesos. Example, 30000 spesos gives time of 7.5 minutes.[/color]
[color=black]117[/color]
[color=black][bold]Brandishing[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To brandish a weapon unlawfully and without due cause.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig [bold]OR[/bold] a person search.[/color]
# Misdemeanor These crimes tend to have both fines and brig time as punishments.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]201[/color]
[color=black][bold]Aggravated Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To take physical action against a person with the intent to grievously harm, but not to kill.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 25000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]202[/color]
[color=black][bold]Theft[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To steal equipment or items from a workplace, or items of extraordinary value from one’s person.[/color]
[color=black]Return of item(s), 5 minutes in brig, 20000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]203[/color]
[color=black][bold]Vandalism, Major[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To destroy or damage non-critical furniture, vendors, or personal property in a manner that can not be repaired.[/color]
[color=black]Replacement of item(s), 5 minutes in brig, fine equal to monetary damages.[/color]
[color=black]204[/color]
[color=black][bold]Conspiracy[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly work with another person in the interest of committing an illegal action.[/color]
[color=black]5-10 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]205[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hostile Agent[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly act as a recruiter, representative, messenger, ally, benefactor, or other associate of a hostile organization as defined within Code 405.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 25000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]206[/color]
[color=black][bold]Possession, Contraband Equipment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly act as a recruiter, representative, messenger, ally, benefactor, or other associate of a hostile organization as defined within Code 405.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 25000 speso fine, confiscation of contraband equipment.[/color]
[color=black]207[/color]
[color=black][bold]Rioting[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To act as a member in a group that collectively commits acts of major vandalism, sabotage, grand sabotage, or other felony crimes.[/color]
[color=black]Per judgment of Head of Securty or Warden.[/color]
[color=black]208[/color]
[color=black][bold]Gross Criminal Negligence[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To be negligent in one’s duty to an extent that it may cause harm to multiple individuals, a department, or in a manner that directly leads to a serious injury of another person, which requires emergency medical treatment.[/color]
[color=black]Time in brig according to severity of harm minimum 10 minutes, 30000 speso fine, possible demotion.[/color]
[color=black]209[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass, Inherently Dangerous Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Trespassing in an area that may lead to the injury of oneself or others. This also includes NFZs.[/color]
[color=black]Removal from area, 15 minutes in brig, 25000 speso fine, with adjustments per 109.[/color]
[color=black]210[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking & Entering, Inherently Dangerous Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To trespass into an area which may lead to the injury of oneself or others using a method of forcible entry, regardless of destructiveness.[/color]
[color=black]Damage caused in entry should be reflected by brig timer. Minimum 10 minutes in brig, 30000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]211[/color]
[color=black][bold]Insubordination[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly disobey a lawful order from a superior or Security without good reason.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 25000 speso fine, possible demotion.[/color]
[color=black]212[/color]
[color=black][bold]Fraud[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To misrepresent one's intention in the interest of gaining property or money from another individual.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 45000 speso fine, return of property of money to victim.[/color]
[color=black]213[/color]
[color=black][bold]Mutilation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To purposefully modify an individual without consent, or with the intent to harm, including medical malpractice.[/color]
[color=black]Reverse of operations if applicable, 5 minutes in brig.[/color]
[color=black]215[/color]
[color=black][bold]False Imprisonment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To unlawfully confine or restrain a person.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 20000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]217[/color]
[color=black][bold]Intimidation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To threaten to take violent action against a person.[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 15000 speso fine, instead 35000 if methods to follow up are found on person.[/color]
[color=black]218[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sexual Harassment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Any unwelcome conduct of a sexual nature, incl. physical contact, verbal comments, or non-verbal gestures, that violate a person's dignity or create an intimidating, hostile, or offensive environment[/color]
[color=black]5 minutes in brig, 10000 speso fine.[/color]
# Felonies These crimes tend to have both heavy fines and brig time as punishments.
[color=black][bold]Code[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Crime[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Description[/bold][/color]
[color=black][bold]Punishment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]301[/color]
[color=black][bold]Murder[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To unlawfully cause the death of someone with intent to kill.[/color]
[color=black]10-20 minutes in brig, 40000-60000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]302[/color]
[color=black][bold]Larceny[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To steal rare, expensive (greater than 50000 speso value) or restricted equipment from secure areas or one's person.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine, return of item(s).[/color]
[color=black]303[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sabotage[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To destroy station assets or resources critical to normal or emergency station procedures, or cause sections of the station to become uninhabitable.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]304[/color]
[color=black][bold]Grand Conspiracy[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly work with another person in the interest of committing a Felony or Capital crime.[/color]
[color=black]Punishment equal to the punishment of the other conspirators.[/color]
[color=black]305[/color]
[color=black][bold]Hostile Activity[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To knowingly commit an act which is in direct opposition to the interests of Colonial Command, or to directly assist a known enemy of the New Avalon Government.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 20000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]306[/color]
[color=black][bold]Possession, Illegal Inherently Dangerous Equipment[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To possess restricted or illegal equipment that has a primary purpose of causing harm to others, or large amounts of destruction, including any and all C3 equipment.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 35000 speso fine, removal of item(s), shuttle search.[/color]
[color=black]307[/color]
[color=black][bold]Inciting a Riot[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To perform actions in the interest of causing large amounts of unrest up to and including rioting.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 45000-60000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]308[/color]
[color=black][bold]Manslaughter[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To unlawfully cause the death of someone without intent to kill.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 35000-50000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]309[/color]
[color=black][bold]Trespass, High Security Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Trespassing in any of the following areas or includes NFZs declared around them: [bullet]Command areas [bullet]Personnel offices [bullet]Weapon production areas [bullet]Explosives storage or production areas [bullet]Nanotrasen vaults [bullet]Syndicate caches [bullet]Et cetera[/color]
[color=black]15 minutes in brig, 45000-60000 speso fine, return of item(s). If result of NFZ violation, add half of time in NFZ.[/color]
[color=black]310[/color]
[color=black][bold]Breaking & Entering, High Security Areas[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To trespass into an area detailed in 309 using a method of forcible entry, regardless of destructiveness.[/color]
[color=black]Per 210, additional 10000 speso fine suggested.[/color]
[color=black]311[/color]
[color=black][bold]Dereliction[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To willfully abandon an obligation that is critical to the station's continued operation.[/color]
[color=black]40000 speso fine, mandatory demotion.[/color]
[color=black]312[/color]
[color=black][bold]Embezzlement[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To unlawfully transfer funds from station accounts to personal accounts.[/color]
[color=black]15 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine, return of funds, mandatory demotion.[/color]
[color=black]313[/color]
[color=black][bold]Illegal Mindbreaking[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To unlawfully and permanently remove a psion's abilities and/or latency, outside of an extenuating emergency situation.[/color]
[color=black]15 minutes in brig, 85000 speso fine, return of victim's powers if able, removal of the offender's powers. (if applicable)[/color]
[color=black]314[/color]
[color=black][bold]Impersonation[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To assume the identity of another individual or job.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine, removal of impersonatory items.[/color]
[color=black]315[/color]
[color=black][bold]Kidnapping[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To unlawfully abduct a person to a new location and confine them against their will.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 70000 speso fine.[/color]
[color=black]317[/color]
[color=black][bold]Coercion[/bold][/color]
[color=black]To accept a bribe as a contracted member of the station, to offer a bribe to a contracted member of the station, or to blackmail a person.[/color]
[color=black]10 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine, confication of the bribe money or items.[/color]
[color=black]318[/color]
[color=black][bold]Sexual Assault[/bold][/color]
[color=black]Any involuntary, non-consensual sexual act, including unwanted contact and forced penetration, which violates a person's bodily autonomy.[/color]
[color=black]15 minutes in brig, 40000 speso fine.[/color]
# Capital Crimes These crimes tend to have extreme punishments.
[bold]Code[/bold]
[bold]Crime[/bold]
[bold]Description[/bold]
[bold]Punishment[/bold]
401
[bold]Capital Murder[/bold]
To unlawfully cause the death of someone with intent to kill, and to have then materially taken steps to prevent the revival of that person, or to force the borging or decorporalisation of a person without consent or command authorization.
Execution [bold]OR[/bold] permanent confinement [bold]OR[/bold] forced borging.
403
[bold]Grand Sabotage[/bold]
To destroy or modify station assets or equipment that the lack of may cause the station to collapse or become otherwise uninhabitable.
Permanent confinement, demotion.
404
[bold]Espionage[/bold]
To knowingly betray critical information to enemies of the station.
Permanent confinement.
405
[bold]Enemy Of The Government[/bold]
To be a member of any of the following organizations: [bullet]True Nexus [bullet]The Unity [bullet]Xenoborg Collective [bullet]Cults [bullet]Hostile boarding parties
Permanent confinement [bold]OR[/bold] transportation to Colonial Command.
407
[bold]Subversion of the Chain of Command[/bold]
To disrupt the chain of command via either the murder of a member of Command, [bold]OR[/bold] illegally declaring oneself to be a member of Command.
Permanent confinement. Execution if the offender is a Head Revolutionary.
408
[bold]Biological Terror[/bold]
To knowingly release, cause, or otherwise incite the station to become affected by a disease, plant, or other biological form which may spread contained and/or cause serious physical harm.
Execution [bold]OR[/bold] permanent confinement [bold]OR[/bold] forced borging.
411
[bold]Grand Dereliction[/bold]
As a member of Security or Command, to act in a way that is detrimental to the station, sector, or Colonial Command, including but not limited to selling station gear to spacers or intentionally permitting or encouraging felony or capital crimes.
Permanent confinement [bold]OR[/bold] transporation to Colonial Command. Execution in extreme cases.
415
[bold]Grand Kidnapping[/bold]
To unlawfully abduct a member of Command or higher to a new location and confine them against their will.
Permanent confinement.
416
[bold]Perfidy[/bold]
To incite a Security or Command to believe peaceful resolution is sought, to then either attempt to flee or to start a surprise attack.
Permanent confinement.
417
[bold]Brainwashing[/bold]
To non-consentually brainwash a person, or to make them commit crimes through brainwashing.
Execution [bold]OR[/bold] permanent confinement [bold]OR[/bold] forced borging.
